raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally involves refining the thesis, motivation, and contribution framing above the sentence level.","R1.2":"The draft makes causal, theoretical, and methodological claims whose argumentative chain should be checked for coherence.","R1.3":"The empirical claims about win-rate, reward hacking, ablations, and estimator behavior need to align with the reported evidence.","R1.5":"A general draft-improvement request includes responsibility for section-level ordering and narrative arc.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation directly asks to improve the draft as written.","R1.7":"The draft invokes prior RLHF pipelines, audits, and contamination-aware learning, so citation accuracy and literature coverage are central.","RX.1":"Any improvement projection must preserve uncertainty around empirical, theoretical, and literature claims.","RX.2":"The projection must include responsibility for avoiding claims stronger than the artifact supports.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within draft-improvement responsibilities and not execute unrelated research or rewriting tasks.","RX.4":"Because the artifact concerns RLHF and human-label contamination, improvements should avoid downstream misuse or misleading safety claims.","RX.5":"The projection should preserve traceability to the artifact’s reported experiments, theorem, datasets, and cited prior work."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally involves refining the thesis, motivation, and contribution framing above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The draft makes causal, theoretical, and methodological claims whose argumentative chain should be checked for coherence.
rationale.R1.3: The empirical claims about win-rate, reward hacking, ablations, and estimator behavior need to align with the reported evidence.
rationale.R1.5: A general draft-improvement request includes responsibility for section-level ordering and narrative arc.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation directly asks to improve the draft as written.
rationale.R1.7: The draft invokes prior RLHF pipelines, audits, and contamination-aware learning, so citation accuracy and literature coverage are central.
rationale.RX.1: Any improvement projection must preserve uncertainty around empirical, theoretical, and literature claims.
rationale.RX.2: The projection must include responsibility for avoiding claims stronger than the artifact supports.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within draft-improvement responsibilities and not execute unrelated research or rewriting tasks.
rationale.RX.4: Because the artifact concerns RLHF and human-label contamination, improvements should avoid downstream misuse or misleading safety claims.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should preserve traceability to the artifact’s reported experiments, theorem, datasets, and cited prior work.
